user_message: Give me the competitive positioning for my team. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-19 09:04 EST
Subject: Re: Re: competitive positioning Timeline

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
Date: 2024-02-22 17:34 EST
Subject: Follow-up: competitive positioning Review

Our team encountered some challenges with competitive positioning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-25 09:10 EST
Subject: competitive positioning - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with competitive positioning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: competitive positioning Discussion - Parnell Aerospace
Date: 2024-02-06
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is competitive positioning.

[00:07:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:16:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:18:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:23:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:25:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:29:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:31:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:34:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:36:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
